title: Brookline voters overwhelmingly approve tax override to avoid school, public safety cuts
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Brookline voters approved a tax override, with 8,859 votes for and 5,826 against, avoiding public safety and school cuts.
The tax override will raise $23 million, increasing property taxes by 18% over three years, aiding schools and municipal services.
Failure to pass the override could have led to cuts of 240 positions, affecting education programs and public safety staffing.

title: Do the Bruins have a potential No. 1 center already on their roster?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins management identifies need for a true No. 1 center to contend in a competitive Atlantic Division.
Young players Fraser Minten and James Hagens seen as potential future top-line centers for the Bruins.
Team faces choices between patience with player development and expensive trades for established centers.

title: Maine police arrest victim’s boyfriend for 1987 murder
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Maine police arrested Stephen Bouchard, 63, for the 1987 murder of his girlfriend, Alice Hawkes.
Alice Hawkes was found dead in her Westbrook apartment, after Bouchard reported being locked out on October 4, 1987.
A 2025 reinvestigation by Maine's Major Crimes Unit led to Bouchard facing murder charges nearly 40 years later.

title: Is Patriots’ 2026 schedule actually easier than many expected? This win-projection metric says so.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The New England Patriots are projected to have the 12th-easiest NFL schedule in 2026, according to Sharp Football Analysis.
The Patriots' 2026 opponents include division champs like the Seahawks and Jaguars, and tough teams like the Chiefs and Chargers.
Despite some formidable opponents, matchups with lower-ranked teams such as the Jets and Dolphins contribute to an easier schedule ranking.

title: Could Patriots reunite with Stefon Diggs? One Patriots writer doesn’t expect it.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Stefon Diggs, former Patriots wideout, remains a free agent despite strong 2025 season with 1,045 yards.
Patriots reporter Phil Perry doubts a Diggs return, citing potential trade for A.J. Brown as a stronger option.
Diggs' release was driven by salary cap considerations, not the court case, as the Patriots improve their roster.

title: Man, 31, killed in New Bedford shooting
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Coan, 31, died from gunshot wounds after being found in his car in New Bedford.
ShotSpotter detected gunshots near Acushnet Avenue and Russell Street on May 6 around 11:30 p.m.
State Police and New Bedford police are investigating the incident as a homicide.

title: Hayden, Rollins, and third Suffolk DA candidate Champion secure enough signatures to appear on ballot
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kevin Hayden, Rachael Rollins, and Linda Champion qualified for the Suffolk DA primary ballot by gathering over 1,000 signatures each.
In Suffolk County communities, Hayden, Champion, and Rollins collected varying numbers of signatures, with Rollins leading in totals.
Rollins, Hayden, and Champion have different financial standings for the campaign, with Hayden holding the most funds at over $60,000.

title: These are Boston’s highest-paid tech CEOs, BBJ data shows
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Wayfair CEO Niraj Shah received $280.6 million in stock awards in 2025, topping Boston's highest-paid tech CEOs list.
Vincent Roche of Analog Devices ranked second with a total compensation of $27,456,265, according to Boston Business Journal.
Neil Barua of PTC Inc. was third with $24,315,129 in earnings, based on filings with the U.S. SEC.

title: Karen Read’s SUV heads to auction in Woburn. Here’s what readers think it’s worth.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Karen Read's 2021 Lexus LX 570, central to her murder trial, will be auctioned in Woburn on May 14.
Boston.com readers predict the SUV auction bids could range from under $70,000 to over $500,000.
The SUV has undergone repairs after storage damage and has approximately 12,000 miles, affecting its auction value.

title: ‘What happened today cannot stand’: Memorial Drive shooter fired more than 50 rounds before trooper, civilian intervened, DA says
author: Abby Patkin and Molly Farrar
bullets: Tyler Brown, 46, fired more than 50 rounds on Memorial Drive, injuring two men before being shot by authorities.
A State Police trooper and a Marine veteran civilian shot Brown, ending the active shooter situation in minutes.
Brown is charged with two counts of armed assault with intent to murder and firearm offenses, now in custody at a hospital.
